📖 English

Comprehension and Composition

Learn to read carefully and write beautifully!

📚 Chapter 8 ⏱️ 2 min read

Section B: Comprehension and Composition

This section helps you understand what you read and express what you think.

Reading Comprehension

Reading comprehension means understanding a passage and answering questions about it.

Steps for Good Comprehension

  1. Read the passage carefully - twice if needed.
  2. Underline key words and main ideas.
  3. Read the questions first (sometimes).
  4. Find answers in the passage.
  5. Write answers in your own words.

Sample Passage

The elephant is the largest land animal on Earth. It has a long trunk, large ears, and two white tusks. Elephants are very intelligent and have good memories. They live in herds led by the oldest female. Elephants eat leaves, grass, fruits, and bark.

Sample Questions

  1. What is the largest land animal?
  2. What do elephants eat?
  3. Who leads an elephant herd?

Tips

  • Do not leave questions blank.
  • If the passage says it, it is the answer!
  • Write complete sentences when asked.

Paragraph Writing

A paragraph is a group of sentences about one main idea.

Structure of a Paragraph

PartWhat it doesExample
Topic SentenceTells the main ideaMy school is a wonderful place.
Supporting DetailsGives facts and examplesIt has a big library. The teachers are kind.
Concluding SentenceWraps up the ideaI am proud to study there.

Sample Paragraph (Topic: My Pet)

My pet dog’s name is Tommy. He is brown and very friendly. Every morning, he wags his tail and waits for me. I take him for a walk after school. Tommy is my best friend.

Essay Writing

An essay is a longer piece of writing with multiple paragraphs.

Basic Essay Structure

PartContent
Introduction (1 paragraph)Introduce the topic and grab attention
Body (2-3 paragraphs)Give details, examples, and explanations
Conclusion (1 paragraph)Summarize and give a final thought

Common Essay Topics for Class 5

  • My Best Friend
  • My Favorite Holiday
  • The Importance of Trees
  • A Visit to a Zoo
  • My Ambition in Life

Sample: “My Best Friend” (Introduction)

A true friend is a gift in life. My best friend is Riya Sharma. She sits next to me in class. I am lucky to have her as my friend.

Letter Writing

Formal Letter (to principal, teacher, officer)

Format:

🎯

I read this note!

Tap the button when you are done to earn a sticker!
